scenes.narration: Your phone recognizes your face. Your email filters spam. Your feed knows what you'll watch. They all learned — without being programmed.
scenes.visual_spec: Icons appear in sequence: phone with face outline, email envelope with shield, play button with sparkles. Each pulses on its keyword, then all three orbit a central question mark that morphs into a brain.

scenes.narration: Traditional programming is rules. If this, then that. A human writes every instruction.
scenes.visual_spec: Code block appears with 'if/else' statements. Arrows flow top-to-bottom through the logic. A stick figure labeled 'Developer' types the rules. Mechanical, rigid feel.

scenes.narration: Machine Learning flips it. You give it examples — thousands of them — and it figures out the rules itself.
scenes.visual_spec: The code block shrinks and moves aside. A flood of data points (images, numbers, text snippets) streams in from the left. They funnel into a box labeled 'Model'. The box glows and outputs 'RULES' on the right. Visual flip/transformation moment.

scenes.narration: Show it ten thousand cats, ten thousand dogs. It learns the patterns — ears, fur, shape — until it can tell them apart on its own.
scenes.visual_spec: Split screen: left side shows cat photos cascading, right shows dog photos. A neural network icon in the center 'absorbs' them. Feature callouts appear: pointy ears, whiskers, snout shapes. A new unlabeled photo enters, the model outputs 'CAT' with a checkmark.

scenes.narration: That's Machine Learning: teaching computers to learn from data, not from instructions.
scenes.visual_spec: Clean summary frame. Text 'Machine Learning' in large primary color. Below it: two columns — crossed-out 'Instructions' on left, glowing 'Data → Patterns → Predictions' flow on right. Minimal, memorable.
